title: Ensure that all Namespaces have Network Policies defined

input_row.title: Ensure that all Namespaces have Network Policies defined
input_row.description: Use network policies to isolate traffic in your cluster network.
input_row.rationale: Running different applications on the same Kubernetes cluster creates a risk of one compromised application attacking a neighboring application. Network segmentation is important to ensure that containers can communicate only with those they are supposed to. A network policy is a specification of how selections of pods are allowed to communicate with each other and other network endpoints. Once there is any Network Policy in a namespace selecting a particular pod, that pod will reject any connections that are not allowed by any Network Policy. Other pods in the namespace that are not selected by any Network Policy will continue to accept all traffic
input_row.audit: The OpenShift 4 CNI plugin uses network policies and by default all Pods in a project are accessible from other Pods and network endpoints. To isolate one or more Pods in a project, you create NetworkPolicy objects in that project to indicate the allowed incoming connections. Project administrators can create and delete NetworkPolicy objects within their own project. For more information see: Run the following command and review the NetworkPolicy objects created in the cluster. oc -n all get networkpolicy Ensure that each namespace defined in the cluster has at least one Network Policy.
input_row.remediation: Follow the documentation and create NetworkPolicy objects as you need them. Internal Only - General
input_row.impact: Once there is any Network Policy in a namespace selecting a particular pod, that pod will reject any connections that are not allowed by any Network Policy. Other pods in the namespace that are not selected by any Network Policy will continue to accept all traffic"
input_row.default_value: By default, all Pods in a project are accessible from other Pods and network endpoints; network policies are not created.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The description and rationale of the control indicate importance for network policies within a Kubernetes cluster, which is a standard part of OpenShift's configuration. 
- The audit field provides a specific command (`oc -n all get networkpolicy`) which can be translated to API checks using the OpenShift APIs.
- Given that the audit mentions a command that can check the existence of network policies, it indicates both Cypher and CLI support for querying, satisfying the criteria for an automated approach.
- The rationale primarily focuses on technical configurations, rather than organizational policy, making it suitable for automation.

**PROGRAM NAME:** ibm_openshift_namespace_network_policy_defined

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the IBM Cloud CLI or OpenShift CLI calls to check for network policies across all namespaces.
- The command to be executed would be analogous to running: `oc get networkpolicy --all-namespaces`
- This command will enumerate all the NetworkPolicy objects in the OpenShift cluster, and you'll specifically check if each namespace has at least one NetworkPolicy defined.
- Validate that for each namespace (`namespace`) in the cluster, the count of NetworkPolicy objects should be greater than zero.
- Clear Pass/Fail criteria:
   - Pass: If each namespace has at least one NetworkPolicy object.
   - Fail: If any namespace has zero NetworkPolicy objects, indicating it does not comply with the CIS benchmark.

This program leverages the OpenShift APIs to ensure that each namespace has network policies defined, thereby enhancing security through traffic isolation.
